steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Command of Evidence (Quantitative) question. The student is surprised the ColecoVision sold "relatively few units." The correct answer must cite the ColecoVision's actual unit count from the table and compare it to another system that sold significantly more, making the surprise understandable.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 3,000,000 units sold compared to the approximately 4,487,000 units sold of the Game & Watch.: Both figures are wrong. The table shows ColecoVision at 2,000,000 units, not 3,000,000, and Game & Watch at 18,600,000 units, not 4,487,000. ✗
  • Choice B — 2,000,000 units sold compared to the approximately 18,600,000 units sold of the Game & Watch.: Both figures match the table exactly. The stark gap — 2 million vs. 18.6 million — explains why the student is surprised that ColecoVision sold so few. ✓
  • Choice C — 2,000,000 units sold compared to the approximately 18,600,000 units sold of the Intellivision.: The ColecoVision figure is correct, but the table shows the Intellivision sold far fewer than 18,600,000 units; 18,600,000 is the Game & Watch's figure. ✗
  • Choice D — 3,000,000 units sold compared to the approximately 4,487,000 units sold of the Apple II.: The ColecoVision figure is wrong (should be 2,000,000), and 4,487,000 is not the Apple II's correct table value. ✗
That matches Choice B — 2,000,000 units sold compared to the approximately 18,600,000 units sold of the Game & Watch..
